reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — real transformers.js@3.7.5 image-classification pipeline for onnx-community/swin-finetuned-food101-ONNX ran in a Web Worker (worker.js processor→model forward pass), WASM, q8. Fresh-profile end-to-end: Download (~93 MB) → ready → Identify returned real Food-101 labels on the pizza sample: Pizza 99.8%, Lasagna 0.1%, Gnocchi, Bruschetta, Hummus (513 ms). Sushi sample: Sushi 95.4%, Sashimi 4.5%, Scallops, Ceviche, Spring rolls (515 ms). conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass. screenshots: reports/verify/food/results-desktop.png, results-mobile.png, results-light.png.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT food domain (101 dish classes), not generic ImageNet ViT. Runnability pre-verified before build: standalone dynamic-import of the CDN build returned pizza 0.9983 on the same photo.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output.logits is a [1,101] tensor; softmax over 101 classes sums to 1; top-k are semantically correct dishes and runners-up are visual neighbours (sushi→sashimi, pizza→lasagna/gnocchi). See-inside table shows raw logit next to softmax %, plus top-1 margin (99.8 pts on pizza) and normalised entropy (0.004 on pizza, i.e. very peaky) with a confident/uncertain verdict + confidence needle. conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: 101-class Food-101 label set confirmed from the model config id2label; snake_case labels pretty-printed for display.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/swin-finetuned-food101-ONNX linked to card), declares-task=pass (image-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker in food.js). At-a-glance also states Swin base architecture, Food-101 labels, model_quantized.onnx, ~93 MB, Apache-2.0.

Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'This model isn't on your device yet.' + a 'Download model (~93 MB)' button (NO silent auto-download). After download the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready — running locally on your device.' with a 'Clear cached model' control. Second page load in the SAME browser (Cache Storage persists) auto-initialised straight to ready with state transition just 'ready' and NO Download/Re-download button — only 'Clear cached model' present. conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual (agent verdict recorded via /tmp verify-cache run).
rubric.notes: Agent-verified on both device classes via the shared lib/model-loader.js createModelLoader architecture.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — streamed download progress via <progress>; the shared loader surfaces Retry on load failure; classification errors go to a .status.err region ('Classification failed: …'); q8 ONNX cached in Cache Storage for offline reuse; runs on the WASM path with no GPU (headless no-GPU produced real labels). conformance: live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.

Agent verdict — Identify button, top-k range slider (live label), keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails (Enter/Space, role=button, aria-label), and on the ladder pages: batch tagging + nutrition cards (practical), zoom/rotate/occlusion sliders + Reset (wild), dual-loader Name & describe (multimodel) all operable. conformance: controls-operable=manual.

Agent verdict — every visible dish label + probability comes from the on-device Swin run (verified real output: pizza 99.8%, sushi 95.4%/sashimi 4.5%). The practical page's calorie/macro figures are clearly labelled as a static reference-table estimate keyed on the predicted dish (NOT model output, NOT medical advice); the dish label itself is real. conformance: no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + run status; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow/aria-label; labelled top-k slider; keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails; long dish labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere with min-inline-size:0 so bar heads never overflow.

Agent verdict — responsive-check programmatic pass on BOTH classes: desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 both clean (overflow ≤1px, 0 clipped controls, 0 sub-24px targets, console+network clean). Dark mobile screenshot shows the at-a-glance model-id cell wrapping cleanly with no horizontal overflow; light desktop screenshot shows the full run panel + bars legible. conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/food-classification/{desktop,mobile}.png + reports/verify/food/results-*.png.
rubric.notes: Agent-verified on both device classes; models.json support set desktop:ok, mobile:ok.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — all inference (RawImage.read + processor + model forward) runs in a Web Worker; the main thread only renders bars + the see-inside table. Batch tagging (practical) awaits one worker round-trip per image (a natural yield point), so a large batch never blocks the UI. Single classify ~510 ms WASM in the worker; 0 console errors during load or run. conformance: inp-stays-low=manual.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two column layout without horizontal overflow for an image classification demo
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex-wrap:wrap and flex:1 1 <basis>; set min-inline-size:0 on flex items containing long unbreakable content so they shrink instead of overflowing; use logical properties and intrinsic sizing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run panel is a .field-row flex container with two flex:1 1 280px columns each carrying min-inline-size:0; bar labels use overflow-wrap:anywhere; the at-a-glance/see-inside tables are inline-size:100%. responsive-check found overflow ≤1px and 0 clipped controls on both classes.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: reports/responsive/screens/food-classification/desktop.png + mobile.png; dark mobile shot shows the long model-id cell wrapping without overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work web worker off main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread; yield periodically so the browser can handle input and paint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all model load + inference run in a Web Worker (worker.js); the batch-tagging loop in the practical page awaits one worker result per image, which yields the main thread between images rather than running a single blocking loop.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless: classify ~510 ms in the worker, main-thread eval responsive during load, 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload dropzone keyboard operable range slider live status region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; make custom controls keyboard-operable with roles; announce state in a live region; expose meaningful values on progress meters.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dropzone is role=button tabindex=0 with Enter/Space handlers + aria-label; sample thumbs same; loader + run status are role=status aria-live=polite; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow + descriptive aria-label; top-k slider is wrapped in a <label>.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; the food pages link it and use the shared design system (light+dark, WCAG AA).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; light+dark screenshots both legible

openQuestions: Would a WebGPU execution-provider path (with the honest WASM fallback retained) meaningfully speed up the Swin backbone on capable devices?
Could the practical page swap its static reference table for an on-device nutrition lookup (e.g. an IndexedDB-shipped USDA subset) keyed on the predicted dish?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a Food-101 vs generic ImageNet ViT side-by-side on the same plate, to make the specialist-vs-generalist confidence gap legible.
Add a live-camera 'point at your plate' wild demo that classifies frames from getUserMedia with an honest no-camera fallback.

summary: Revision-1 agent matrix pass for food-classification: a DISTINCT food-domain classifier (onnx-community/swin-finetuned-food101-ONNX, Swin fine-tuned on Food-101, 101 dish classes) — not generic ImageNet ViT. Runnability pre-verified, then a real ~93 MB download + on-device transformers.js WASM q8 run confirmed in headless Chrome (pizza 99.8%, sushi 95.4%/sashimi 4.5%, ~510 ms in a Web Worker). Fresh profile shows an honest Download button; a cached reload auto-inits to ready with no button. See-inside exposes raw logits, softmax, top-1 margin and normalised entropy. Four ladder pages (basics, practical menu/nutrition tagger, wild ambiguous/plated dishes, multimodel classify→describe) all load clean. Light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (no overflow, legible, wrapping cells, ≥44px controls); responsive-check clean on both classes; support set ok/ok. 14/14 auto-checkable conformance assertions expected to pass; 9 manual-evidenced dimensions carry the agent verdicts above.
